caption: The video displays a presentation slide for a course in "CRYOGENIC ENGINEERING," specifically "Tutorial – 4." The content focuses on the "Location of $h_y$ and Additional Work," featuring a formula for $\varepsilon$ and a data table. The table lists values for pressure $p$ in bar, temperature $T$ in Kelvin, enthalpy $h$ in j/g, and entropy $s$ in j/gK across states labeled 1, 2, f, and g. A calculation for $h_i$ is shown as $h_i = 0.9(462-230) + 230 = 438.8\text{ J / g}$. At the bottom of the slide, the text "Prof. M D Atrey, Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Bombay" is visible, alongside an NPTEL logo. As the sequence progresses, a blue highlighted box appears over the final equation: $\frac{W_{add}}{m_{add}} = h_i - h_1 = 462 - 438.8 = 23.2\text{ J / g}$. No people or physical settings are shown.
